_comment: Project-scoped settings shipped with the evolve-loop plugin. Five PreToolUse hooks enforce structural trust boundaries: (1) ship-gate on Bash — only legacy/scripts/lifecycle/ship.sh or the native 'evolve ship' CLI can perform ship-class git/gh ops (v8.13.0). (2) phase-gate on Agent|Task — denies in-process subagent dispatch while a cycle is active, forcing phase agents through the native bridge (rewired per ADR-0075; phase ORDER is enforced by the go/internal/core state machine, not this hook). (3) role-gate on Edit|Write — files can only be modified by the active phase's agent within its allowlisted paths (v8.13.1). (4) research-quota-gate on WebSearch|WebFetch|Bash — counts research-tool calls per agent (v10.16.0). (5) doc-deletion-guard on Bash — blocks rm/mv on docs/** or knowledge-base/** without canonical archival mv (v10.17.0). Each hook prefers the freshly-built go/bin/evolve and falls back to the tracked go/evolve when go/bin is missing, so the kernel fails closed even if build outputs are wiped (the v11.4.0 bash-script dispatch fallback died in the v12 legacy/scripts removal; cycle-243's abort cleanup exposed the gap by deleting go/bin/evolve, which silently disabled all guards). All gates honor an emergency bypass env var; routine bypassing is a CLAUDE.md violation. PostToolUse on Edit|Write syntax-checks the modified file by extension; never blocks (PostToolUse can't), only emits WARN to LLM stderr (v8.13.3).
